canonical_filename.mechanism: SKILL.md (fixed, required filename within the skill directory)

custom_filename.mechanism: skill.mdx is accepted as an alternative to SKILL.md within the skill directory

description.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: description (recommended, not required)

disable_model_invocation.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: disable-model-invocation (optional bool, default: false)

display_name.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: name (optional, defaults to directory name)

user_invocable.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: user-invocable (optional bool, default: true)

providerExtensions.name: Supporting Files
providerExtensions.description: Optional supporting files (scripts, schemas, checklists, etc.) can be co-located in the skill directory alongside SKILL.md. The Droid can reference these files when executing the skill. This enables self-contained skill packages that bundle their own tooling assets.

providerExtensions.name: Auto-Invocation
providerExtensions.description: The Droid automatically invokes matching skills when they apply to the current task, without explicit user prompting. The skill description is the primary trigger signal — the model uses it to determine whether a skill is relevant to the current context. The user-invocable and disable-model-invocation frontmatter keys control whether auto-invocation is permitted.

providerExtensions.name: Monorepo Skill Paths
providerExtensions.description: Skills can be organized per-project within a monorepo using per-project .factory/skills/ directories, or shared across projects via a root-level .factory/skills/ directory. This allows monorepos to maintain project-specific skills alongside shared skills without path conflicts.

providerExtensions.name: Skill Composability
providerExtensions.description: The Droid can chain multiple skills inside a single plan, invoking them in sequence as the plan progresses. This enables composite workflows composed of reusable skill primitives without requiring a single monolithic skill.

providerExtensions.name: Legacy Commands Compatibility
providerExtensions.description: Files under .factory/commands/ continue to work and are recognized by the Droid. Skills (under .factory/skills/) supersede commands as the preferred mechanism, but the commands directory is still loaded for backwards compatibility with pre-skills configurations.
